creator: ae@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Brian Scott from comment #14)
> #10 ck_pr_md_load_ptr (target=0xffff000054dc25e8) at
> /usr/src/sys/contrib/ck/include/gcc/aarch64/ck_pr.h:114
> #11 dyn_free_states (chain=<optimized out>) at
> /usr/src/sys/netpfil/ipfw/ip_fw_dynamic.c:2037
> #12 dyn_tick (vnetx=0x0) at /usr/src/sys/netpfil/ipfw/ip_fw_dynamic.c:2631
> (kgdb) x 0xffff000054dc25e8
> 0xffff000054dc25e8:	Cannot access memory at address 0xffff000054dc25e8
> (kgdb)
> 
> Without knowing any aarch64 assembler, it looks like
> #10 ck_pr_md_load_ptr (target=0xffff000054dc25e8)
> is called with an invalid pointer and thats where things blow up. The
> address doesn't look unreasonable in structure so could just be an out of
> bounds thing. This is loading up the per CPU state in dyn_free_states in the
> DYNSTATE_GET macro:
> 
> CPU_FOREACH(i) {
>        dyn_hp_cache[cached_count] = DYNSTATE_GET(i);
>        if (dyn_hp_cache[cached_count] != NULL)
>            cached_count++;
> }
> 
> My debugging skills are stopped by the optimised out values all over the
> place, so any guidance would be appreciated.

Yes, from previous traces I suspected the problem is in this line, but I don't know why DPCPU_ID_PTR() produces wrong pointer. It looks like CPU_FOREACH() has protection and can not return bad CPU id, but can you try to run the following commands in kgdb:
(kgdb) f 11
(kgdb) i lo
(kgdb) p mp_maxid
(kgdb) p all_cpus
